8af79271e824.summary:
Need for abstinence before fertility treatment questioned as study finds sperm deteriorates as it stays in body

Encouraging men to have more frequent ejaculations may boost their fertility, according to researchers who found that sperm deteriorates over time as it remains in the body.

The longer men went without sex, the more their sperm showed signs of DNA damage and oxidative stress, and the more tests rated the sperm as less viable and poorer swimmers.

9f80952a4db9.title: Just a few minutes of effort could lower your risk of 8 major diseases
9f80952a4db9.summary: Just a few minutes of getting out of breath each day could dramatically cut your risk of major diseases—including heart disease, dementia, and diabetes. A large study of nearly 100,000 people found that it’s not just how much you move, but how intensely you move that matters. Short bursts of vigorous activity—like rushing for a bus or climbing stairs quickly—were linked to striking reductions in disease risk, especially for inflammatory conditions and brain health.

a340512f83fd.title: Scientists shocked to find lab gloves may be skewing microplastics data
a340512f83fd.summary: Scientists may have been unknowingly inflating microplastics pollution estimates, and the surprising source could be their own lab gloves. A University of Michigan study found that common nitrile and latex gloves release tiny particles called stearates, which closely resemble microplastics and can contaminate samples during testing. In some cases, this led to wildly exaggerated results, forcing researchers to track down the unexpected culprit.

03f61623f245.summary: Making babies in space may be more complicated than expected, as new research shows sperm struggle to navigate in microgravity. Scientists found that while sperm can still swim normally, they lose their sense of direction without gravity, making it harder to reach and fertilize an egg. In lab experiments simulating space conditions, far fewer sperm successfully made it through a maze designed to mimic the reproductive tract, and fertilization rates in mice dropped by about 30%.

4493c9fbeb08.title: One of Earth’s most explosive supervolcanoes is recharging
4493c9fbeb08.summary: Far beneath the ocean near Japan, scientists have discovered that the magma system linked to the most powerful eruption of the Holocene is slowly rebuilding. By using seismic imaging, researchers mapped a large magma reservoir under the Kikai caldera and confirmed it is the same system that fueled the massive eruption 7,300 years ago. However, the magma now present is newly injected, not leftover, as shown by changes in the chemistry of recent volcanic material and the growth of a lava dome over

776ca4b48be9.title: Scientists solved the mystery of missing ocean plastic—and the answer is alarming
776ca4b48be9.summary: Scientists have discovered that the ocean’s “missing” plastic hasn’t vanished—it has broken down into trillions of invisible nanoplastics now spread through water, air, and living organisms. These tiny particles may be everywhere, including inside our bodies, raising serious concerns about their impact.

8a1aa34983f5.title: Scientists just found a way to store massive data using light in 3 dimensions
8a1aa34983f5.summary: A new holographic storage technique uses light in three dimensions to dramatically increase how much data can be stored. It encodes information throughout a material using amplitude, phase, and polarization, rather than just on a surface. An AI model then reconstructs the data from light patterns, simplifying the process. This could pave the way for faster, denser, and more efficient data storage systems.

beb1416dc8c8.title: Popular sugar substitute linked to brain damage and stroke risk
beb1416dc8c8.summary: A widely used sugar substitute found in everything from keto snacks to diet drinks may not be as harmless as it seems. New research shows that erythritol can disrupt brain blood vessel cells, reducing their ability to relax, increasing harmful oxidative stress, and impairing the body’s ability to break down clots. These changes create conditions that could raise stroke risk, even at typical consumption levels.

0a8a3c3d5124.title: After 20 years, scientists finally explain the Crab Pulsar’s strange “zebra stripes”
0a8a3c3d5124.summary: Ученые спустя 20 лет объяснили загадочные "зебра stripes" в радиоизлучении пульсара Кабана. Новое исследование предполагает, что эти полосы возникли из-за взаимодействия гравитации и плазмы: плазма рассеивает свет, а гравитация возвращает его, создавая интерференционные волны.
0a8a3c3d5124.original_description: For decades, astronomers have been puzzled by strange “zebra stripe” patterns in radio waves from the Crab Pulsar — bright bands separated by complete darkness. Now, new research suggests the answer lies in a cosmic tug-of-war between gravity and plasma. The pulsar’s plasma spreads light apart, while gravity bends it back together, creating interference patterns that form the striking stripes.

ebc946a6a981.title: World's smallest QR code, smaller than bacteria, could store data for centuries
ebc946a6a981.summary: Scientists have created a microscopic QR code so tiny it can only be seen with an electron microscope—smaller than most bacteria and now officially a world record. But this isn’t just about size; it’s about durability. By engraving data into ultra-stable ceramic materials, the team has opened the door to storing information that could last for centuries or even millennia without needing power or maintenance.

4112531a0721.title: What teens eat could be affecting their mental health more than we thought
4112531a0721.summary: What teens eat might matter more for their mental health than previously thought. A sweeping review of nearly 20 studies found that healthier diets are often linked to fewer depressive symptoms, while poor eating habits may go hand in hand with greater psychological distress. Interestingly, focusing on whole dietary patterns—not just individual nutrients—showed more consistent benefits, suggesting that overall eating habits could play a meaningful role during this critical stage of brain develop

39ff2681f525.title: This quantum computing breakthrough may not be what it seemed
39ff2681f525.summary: A team of physicists set out to test some of the most exciting claims in quantum computing—and found a very different story. Instead of confirming breakthroughs, their careful replication studies revealed that signals once hailed as major advances could actually be explained in simpler ways. Despite the importance of these findings, their work initially struggled to get published, highlighting a deeper issue in science.

6a948b78dc32.title: This new therapy turns off pain without opioids or addiction
6a948b78dc32.summary: Ученые разработали новую генную терапию, которая блокирует болевые сигналы в мозге, не используя опиоиды и связанные с ними риски зависимости.  Технология, основанная на анализе работы мозга с помощью ИИ, создает "выключатель" боли, имитирующий эффект морфина, но без побочных эффектов.  Ранние испытания показали длительное облегчение боли без нарушения нормальных ощущений, что открывает перспективы для безопасных и не вызывающих привыкания методов лечения.
6a948b78dc32.original_description: Scientists have developed a new gene therapy that quiets pain at its source in the brain—without the addictive risks of opioids. Using AI to map how pain is processed, they created a targeted “off switch” that mimics morphine’s benefits but skips its dangerous side effects. In early tests, it delivered lasting relief without affecting normal sensations. The discovery could mark a major step toward safer, non-addictive pain treatments.

d4bace17b448.title: Stroke triggers a hidden brain change that looks like rejuvenation
d4bace17b448.summary: After a stroke, the brain may do something surprisingly hopeful—it can “refresh” parts of itself. Researchers analyzing brain scans from over 500 stroke survivors found that while the damaged side of the brain appears to age faster, the opposite, unaffected side can actually look younger. This unexpected shift seems to reflect the brain’s effort to rewire itself, strengthening healthy regions to compensate for lost function.

This unexpected shift seems to reflect the brain’s effort to rewire itself, strengthening healthy regions to compensate for lost function.

03da2e804010.title: New cholesterol guidelines could change when you get tested
03da2e804010.summary: Новые рекомендации по контролю холестерина в США направлены на более раннее и персонализированное профилактическое вмешательство при сердечно-сосудистых заболеваниях.  В рекомендации предлагается начинать скрининг холестерина в более раннем возрасте, иногда даже в детстве, и учитывать не только уровень "плохого" холестерина, но и генетические факторы риска.  Внедряется новая, более точная система оценки риска, использующая широкий спектр данных о здоровье для прогнозирования вероятности инфаркта и инсульта на долгие годы.
03da2e804010.original_description: A major new U.S. cholesterol guideline is shifting the focus toward earlier, more personalized prevention of heart disease. It urges people to start screening sooner—sometimes even in childhood—and highlights the importance of tracking not just LDL (“bad”) cholesterol but also genetic risk factors like lipoprotein(a). A new, more advanced risk calculator now uses broader health data to better predict heart attack and stroke risk over decades.

3148805f2038.title: Scientists say the evidence is clear: E-cigarettes beat patches and gum in helping smokers quit
3148805f2038.summary: Nicotine e-cigarettes may be one of the most effective tools yet for quitting smoking, according to a sweeping review of global research. By analyzing findings from 14 major reviews spanning nearly a decade, researchers found consistent, high-quality evidence showing that nicotine vapes outperform traditional methods like patches, gum, and even behavioral support. While some lower-quality studies produced mixed results, the strongest data clearly favored nicotine e-cigarettes.

While some lower-quality studies produced mixed results, the strongest data clearly favored nicotine e-cigarettes.

2d2674e666f0.title: This hidden state of water could explain why life exists
2d2674e666f0.summary: Ученые обнаружили скрытую критическую точку в сверхмороженой воде, объясняющую ее необычное поведение.  В этой точке сливаются две различные жидкие формы воды, вызывая значительные колебания, влияющие на воду даже при нормальной температуре.  Это открытие, достигнутое благодаря использованию ультра-быстрых рентгеновских лазеров, может пересмотреть понимание роли воды в природе и потенциально в возникновении жизни.
2d2674e666f0.original_description: Scientists have finally found a hidden “critical point” in supercooled water that explains why it behaves so strangely. At this point, two different liquid forms of water merge, triggering powerful fluctuations that affect water even at normal temperatures. The breakthrough was made possible by ultra-fast X-ray lasers that captured water before it froze. This discovery could reshape our understanding of water’s role in nature—and possibly even life itself.

701ea6fc51e6.title: This new carbon material could make carbon capture far more affordable
701ea6fc51e6.summary: Ученые разработали новый углеродный материал, способный значительно удешевить и повысить эффективность улавливания углекислого газа (CO2).  Благодаря контролируемой структуре азота, материал эффективно захватывает и высвобождает CO2 при низких температурах, потенциально используя для работы отработанное тепло. Это открытие открывает новые возможности для разработки передовых климатических технологий.
701ea6fc51e6.original_description: Scientists have created a new kind of carbon material that could make carbon capture much cheaper and more efficient. By carefully controlling how nitrogen atoms are arranged, they found certain structures capture CO2 better and release it using far less heat. One version works at temperatures below 60 °C, meaning it could run on waste heat instead of costly energy. The discovery offers a powerful new blueprint for next-generation climate technology.

7b9fe91a5a4a.title: Scientists discover why your appetite suddenly disappears when you’re sick
7b9fe91a5a4a.summary: Scientists have uncovered how your body actually tells your brain to stop eating when you’re sick. In a new study, researchers found that specialized cells in the gut detect parasites and send signals that ultimately trigger the brain to suppress appetite. This process builds over time, explaining why you may feel fine at first but then suddenly lose interest in food as an infection takes hold.

a676b9054bc3.title: Solar cells just did the “impossible” with this 130% breakthrough
a676b9054bc3.summary: A new solar breakthrough may overcome a long-standing efficiency barrier. Researchers used a “spin-flip” metal complex to capture and multiply energy from sunlight through singlet fission. The result reached about 130% efficiency, meaning more energy carriers were produced than photons absorbed. This could lead to much more powerful solar panels in the future.

597060819a34.title: Scientists discover a hidden system that turns brown fat into a calorie burner
597060819a34.summary: Scientists have identified a key biological system that helps brown fat burn energy by building the networks it needs to function. A protein called SLIT3 splits into two parts, with each piece guiding the growth of blood vessels and nerves inside brown fat. These structures allow the tissue to pull in nutrients and rapidly convert them into heat instead of storing them as fat.

Спасатели несколько дней предпринимали попытки помочь животному добраться до открытого моря, используя лодки и экскаваторы, но кит в итоге смог самостоятельно покинуть мелководье и направиться в свой естественный ареал обитания.
189d4c5a2bc2.original_description:
Rescuers used boats and excavators to try to guide 10-metre long sea mammal to deeper waters

A humpback whale stranded on Germany’s Baltic Sea coast since early this week has freed itself and swum into deeper waters, rescuers said on Friday.

A flotilla of vessels were following the weakened animal at a distance, hoping to help guide it into the North Sea and toward the Atlantic Ocean, its natural habitat.

Эти меры включают штрафы в размере 4,8 млн долларов для предприятия и внедрение новой системы мониторинга качества воздуха.

After stories revealed high levels of contamination in neighborhood around factory processing US toxic waste, government announces sweeping array of tactics

• South Australia 198 & 258; Victoria 261 & 139 | SA win by 56 runs

  • Final day drama as five Victorian wickets fall for just 27 runs

South Australia rifled through Victoria’s lower order to win the Sheffield Shield final and claim back-to-back titles for the first time.

Needing 196 to win, the home side resumed at 102-5 on day five at Junction Oval and were bowled out for 139, giving SA victory by 56 runs.

62ea2d7894b3.summary: Leeds Rhinos got back to winning ways with a 26-22 victory over Warrington Wolves in round six of Super League at AMT Headingley Stadium.
